Value: Garlic Chips
~5 days
prep
~25 mins
Double fryer, burner range, heat lamp or dehydrator

Fine mesh spider, mandolin, deli containers,
sheet trays / resting racks
Allium
QT
Unit: qt, Batch x1: 1, Batch x2: nan, Batch x3: nan, Batch x4: nan, Prep/Brand: thin sliced on mandoline
Slice the garlic on a mandoline as thin as possible, about 0.5mm
In a small pot, add garlic slices and cover with cold water.  Bring to a boil, then strain out water.
Repeat process x2 times.
After third blanch, spread garlic slices on sports towel lined sheet tray to dry
In a preheated Fryer set to ~350°F, fry the chips until golden.
Remove from fryer using a fine mesh spider, reserve to sheet to sheet tray lined with dry sport towel.
Season immediately after frying with kosher salt.
Reserve under a heat lamp or in a warm dry place for at least one hour to remove excess oil.
Reserve in quart containers with C-Fold towel on the bottom and silica gel packets
Store in a cool dry place.
***NOTE: Make as much as is sliced, as needed.  No specific portions to this recipe.
